mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate wage income and household consumption, which lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive during months of positive PAYEMS momentum over a 2-year rolling window, or directional accuracy falls below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals substantial labor market slack that depresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to downward revisions in corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment and falling payroll employment constitute a joint labor market contraction signal that sharply reduces aggregate demand and equity risk appetite, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases month-over-month and PAYEMS decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS fails to produce negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered months, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (incremental R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns only when the federal funds rate is below 1%, because in a near-zero rate regime the equity discount rate is already compressed and marginal growth surprises translate almost entirely into higher equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises and FEDFUNDS is below 1%, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return distribution is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales directly reflects consumer spending strength, the largest component of GDP, and reliably predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth is not associated with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in a 3-year rolling walk-forward test, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3 index points over three months triggers expectations of future monetary tightening and erodes real corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.3 index points over 3 months
failure_condition: After the CPI acceleration trigger fires, 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the mean return is not significantly less than zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts concurrent with a federal funds rate pinned near zero signal a broken credit transmission mechanism and collapsing residential investment, which foreshadows broad demand weakness and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of falling HOUST and FEDFUNDS below 0.25% does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of instances, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and inventory drawdowns that precede broader earnings compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the mean return is not significantly negative.
